questions.text: What is the recommended first segment of a 5-minute Demo Day pitch?

options.text: Technical architecture deep-dive
A personal story or shocking statistic (the Hook)
Tokenomics and revenue model
Listing all MST protocols used

questions.explanation: The first 45 seconds should grab attention emotionally before moving to the problem. Technical details come later.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ B

The first 45 seconds should grab attention emotionally before moving to the problem. Technical details come later.

questions.text: If BridgeKey fails to connect during your live demo, the best immediate action is:

options.text: Keep trying for 2 minutes while apologising
Switch to your pre-recorded backup video and explain you prepared for this scenario
End the presentation early
Blame the MST RPC endpoint

questions.explanation: Preparation for technical failure impresses judges more than a flawless but risky live demo. Always have a backup.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ B

Preparation for technical failure impresses judges more than a flawless but risky live demo. Always have a backup.

questions.text: During your live demo, you should always show:

options.text: Your entire MetaMask transaction history
The confirmed transaction on MST Explorer (mstscan.com)
Your contract’s Solidity code on Remix
A Google Slides mockup of the UI

questions.explanation: Showing the live transaction on the block explorer proves you are on mainnet and builds instant trust.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ B

Showing the live transaction on the block explorer proves you are on mainnet and builds instant trust.

questions.text: When asked "Why MST and not Ethereum?", the best answer focuses on:

options.text: "MST is newer, so it must be better"
Specific MST features you used (e.g., Trika POSA, SARAL, low fees) and how they directly enable your dApp
A generic comparison of market caps
Criticising Ethereum’s development community

questions.explanation: Specificity wins. Mention exactly which MST protocols solved your technical challenges.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ B

Specificity wins. Mention exactly which MST protocols solved your technical challenges.

questions.text: The MST Certified Blockchain Developer certificate is issued as:

options.text: A PDF emailed to you
A soul-bound NFT (SBT) on MST mainnet (Chain ID 4646)
An ERC-20 token that can be traded
A badge on your GitHub profile only

questions.explanation: The certificate is an on-chain, non-transferable soul-bound token minted directly on MST mainnet.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ B

The certificate is an on-chain, non-transferable soul-bound token minted directly on MST mainnet.

questions.text: During Demo Day, it’s acceptable to read directly from a script to ensure you don’t miss any technical details.
You should never mention a pilot partner or potential user unless you have a signed contract.
"If your live demo fails, you should immediately apologise and explain the technical reason in detail."
questions.explanation: 📊 2 marks: FALSE + justification mentioning backup and professionalism.
questions.modelAnswer: 📊 2 marks: FALSE + justification mentioning backup and professionalism.

questions.text: "The MST Demo Day rubric does not consider the business model - only technical implementation matters."
questions.explanation: 📊 2 marks: FALSE + mention of rubric criteria or need for business viability.
questions.modelAnswer: 📊 2 marks: FALSE + mention of rubric criteria or need for business viability.

questions.text: List four things you should have prepared BEFORE stepping onto the Demo Day stage (virtual or physical).
questions.explanation: Backup pre-recorded demo video, pre-funded demo wallet with tokens, clean browser with only the dApp tab open, rehearsed answers to the most common Q&A questions, a slide with contract addresses and Explorer links, a stable internet connection (wired if possible).
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Model Answer (any four)

Backup pre-recorded demo video, pre-funded demo wallet with tokens, clean browser with only the dApp tab open, rehearsed answers to the most common Q&A questions, a slide with contract addresses and Explorer links, a stable internet connection (wired if possible).

2 marks: 0.5 per correct item.

questions.text: Why is it important to show a transaction on MST Explorer during your live demo, even if you already demonstrated the same action in the dApp UI?
questions.explanation: It proves the transaction actually happened on the mainnet - not a mockup or testnet. It builds immediate trust with judges who can verify the details themselves. It also demonstrates that you understand the full stack and are proud of your on-chain footprint.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Model Answer

It proves the transaction actually happened on the mainnet - not a mockup or testnet. It builds immediate trust with judges who can verify the details themselves. It also demonstrates that you understand the full stack and are proud of your on-chain footprint.

2 marks: credibility/trust + mainnet proof.

questions.text:
Write and submit a 5-minute Demo Day script for your capstone project. The script must include:
• A hook (first 45 seconds) that grabs attention.
• Clear problem statement with a real-world statistic or story.
• Description of how your dApp solves it, mentioning at least two MST protocols (e.g., Trika POSA, SARAL, WASMify, FortunaX).
• A planned live demo flow (what you’d show, in what order).
• A closing call to action (what you want from the judges).

Evaluation criteria:
questions.explanation:
2 marks: Hook is compelling and relevant (personal story or statistic).
          1 mark: Clearly mentions specific MST protocols and why they matter.
          1 mark: Demo flow is practical and matches the dApp’s core feature.
          1 mark: Closing includes a specific ask (grant, partnership, mentorship).
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marks Breakdown

2 marks: Hook is compelling and relevant (personal story or statistic).
1 mark: Clearly mentions specific MST protocols and why they matter.
1 mark: Demo flow is practical and matches the dApp’s core feature.
1 mark: Closing includes a specific ask (grant, partnership, mentorship).

Total: 5 marks. Partial credit for missing elements.
